revision.text:
[[ja:伝高畠陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun | image = [[File:Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Kofun
（{{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} of {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|en|Fujiwara no Otomuro|qid=Q2110461|lt=Empress Fujiwara no Otomuro|1=Fujiwara no Otomuro}}'s Takabatakeryo） | alternate_name = | Cluster = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491395|1=Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Mukō|en|Mukō|qid=Q850640|lt=Muko City|1=Mukō}}, Terado-cho Ōmaki | coordinates = {{Coord|34|57|14.77|N|135|41|37.03|E|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter: 65 m; Height: 7 m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = None recorded | Interred_individual = Determined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} to be {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|en|Fujiwara no Otomuro|qid=Q2110461|lt=Empress Fujiwara no Otomuro|1=Fujiwara no Otomuro}} | Mausoleum = Determined as "Takabatakeryo" by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} }} '''Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun''' (伝高畠陵古墳) is a {{ill|Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Mukō|en|Mukō|qid=Q850640|lt=Muko City|1=Mukō}}, Terado-cho Ōmaki, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is one of the tumuli in the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491395|1=Otokuni Kofun Cluster}}. While the actual interred individual is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as the "Takabatakeryo" tomb, believed to be the burial site of {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|en|Fujiwara no Otomuro|qid=Q2110461|lt=Empress Fujiwara no Otomuro|1=Fujiwara no Otomuro}}, consort of Emperor Kanmu (the 50th emperor of Japan). == Overview == [[File:乾垣内遺跡出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shield-shaped haniwa excavated from Inui-Kakitachi Site|qq=}}{{small|Exhibited at the Muko City Cultural Museum.|qq=}}]] This large circular tumulus is located on the eastern slope of the southern part of Mukō Hills (elevation 66 meters) at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10883263}}. Currently, it is under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}, Historic Site1 as the tomb of Fujiwara no Otomuro|lt=Empress Fujiwara no Otomuro, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound has a circular type, with a diameter of approximately 65 meters and a height of 7 meters{{Harvid|Otokuni Kofun Cluster Survey Report|2015|qq=}}. Although its burial goods and structure remain unknown due to the lack of investigations, shield-shaped haniwa were discovered approximately 130 meters northeast at the Inui-Kakitachi site, suggesting a close relationship{{Harvid|Otokuni Kofun Cluster Survey Report|2015|qq=}}. These haniwa, repurposed for coffin use, are noteworthy for their connection to burial goods from {{ill|Konjizuka No. 2 Kofun|ja|黄金塚2号墳|1=Koganezuka No. 2 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konjizuka No. 2 Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganezuka No. 2 Kofun|qq=}} (Kyoto City, Fushimi Ward){{Harvid|Otokuni Kofun Cluster Survey Report|2015|qq=}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late 4th century, during the transitional period from the early to the middle{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}{{Harvid|Otokuni Kofun Cluster Survey Report|2015|qq=}}Muko City Cultural Museum Exhibit Description.. The {{ill|Myōkenyama Kofun|ja|妙見山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōkenyama Kofun|qq=}} previously served as a regional leadership Kofun in the Katsuragawa area, but the transition from keyhole-shaped tumuli to circular tumuli during this period indicates a shift in the political hierarchy. It is believed that Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun fell under the influence of the keyhole-shaped {{ill|Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|en|Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|qid=Q28608917|lt=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}} in Kyoto City's Nishikyō Ward. == Interred_individual == While the actual identity of the interred individual remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates this tumulus as the tomb of '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|en|Fujiwara no Otomuro|qid=Q2110461|lt=Empress Fujiwara no Otomuro|1=Fujiwara no Otomuro}}''', consort of Emperor Kanmu. According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|qid=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'', Fujiwara no Otomuro passed away on the intercalary 10th day of the 3rd month of Enryaku 9 (790). Her funeral was prepared with labor conscripted from neighboring provinces, and she was buried in the "Nagaokayama Mausoleum" on the 28th day of the same month{{Harvid|Fujiwara no Otomuro (National History)|qq=}}{{Harvid|Takabatake Tomb (National History)|qq=}}. The ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}'' lists the "Takabatakeryo" in Otokuni District, Yamashiro Province, as the burial site of the "Empress Dowager of the Fujiwara Clan," with an area of 3 towns eastward, 5 towns westward, 3 towns southward, and 6 towns northward, maintained annually by five households{{Harvid|Takabatake Tomb (National History)|qq=}}. By the medieval period, traditions regarding the tomb's location had faded, but in 1879 (Meiji 12), the present Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun was Historic Site1 as the mausoleum of Fujiwara no Otomuro. Subsequent studies identified it as a tumulus, and the estimated construction date of the mound predates Fujiwara no Otomuro by several centuries. == Related Facilities == * '''Muko City Cultural Museum''' (Muko City, Terado-cho Minamikakitachi): Houses and displays shield-shaped haniwa excavated from the Inui-Kakitachi site. == notes == {{footnotes|qq=}} == References == {{small|(References used for this article)|qq=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title={{ill|National History Encyclopedia|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National History Encyclopedia|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}|isbn=|ref=|qq=}} ''' {{Wikicite|reference=Arikiyo Saeki, "Fujiwara no Otomuro"|ref={{Harvid|Fujiwara no Otomuro (National History)|qq=}}|qq=}}, {{Wikicite|reference=Junichi Tohara, "Takabatake Tomb" (from the Fujiwara no Otomuro entry)|ref={{Harvid|Takabatake Tomb (National History)|qq=}}|qq=}}. * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Empress Kanmu’s Mausoleum|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}} 26: Kyoto Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490263|ref={{Harvid|Empress Kanmu's Mausoleum (Heibonsha)|1981|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=Den-Takabatakeryo Kofun|title=Otokuni Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Otokuni Kofun Cluster Survey Report|2015|qq=}}|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Fujiwara no Otomuro|en|Fujiwara no Otomuro|qid=Q2110461|lt=Fujiwara no Otomuro|1=Fujiwara no Otomuro}} * {{ill|Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|en|Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|qid=Q28608917|lt=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}} * {{ill|Konjizuka No. 2 Kofun|ja|黄金塚2号墳|1=Koganezuka No. 2 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konjizuka No. 2 Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganezuka No. 2 Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|伝高畠陵古墳|version=93924675|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:伝高畠陵古墳]] [[ja:%E4%BC%9D%E9%AB%98%E7%95%A0%E9%99%B5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:%E5%86%86%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q123120842}} [[Category:History of Muko, Kyoto]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]]
